reasoning: The kernel has very low arithmetic intensity at 0.25 FLOP/byte, far below the 62 FLOP/byte ridge point, indicating a bandwidth-dominated workload. DRAM bandwidth utilization is 98.9% of peak (496 GB/s) with 100% global load efficiency, so the kernel is already saturating memory throughput rather than being limited by compute, occupancy, or register spills.
